what_it_does: Records the outcome and by-use verification of the JUDGE build lane, which added roles/judge_mining.py — the confirm-gate validator for the mining composition (③). Reports the single file written, the new role id, a discovery/floor/adversarial/faithful verification narrative, status, and design notes.
observations: Discovery shows judge_mining live with exactly three output fields ['grounded','unsupported','confidence'], mode_scope={'mining'}, input_addresses=('utterance',), op=generate, in the mining cast and not the listening cast.
Floor: cognition_governance_acceptance 42/42 PASS; the AST scan confirms it is pure-declarative (no resolve/approve/dispatch, no claude -p).
Adversarial fire: an extract fabricating a tim_correction over an unrelated GPU-residency Q&A returned grounded:false, naming the exact ungrounded field, confidence 1.0.
Faithful fire: an extract whose every non-empty field is supported returned grounded:true, empty unsupported, confidence 1.0, with no spurious failure on empty fields.

Design: mirrors verify_lens.py as a single-generate validator (deliberately NOT a draws:N jury, distinct from confirm_registration/verify_jury siblings); the {extract, raw_exchange} pair rides in the unit at ctx['utterance'].
Prompt posture biases toward FAIL (false-negative is the dangerous error for a no-fiction gate) and explicitly exempts empty '' fields from being counted unsupported.
needs_tim flags honest limits: it is a single-4B soft judgement (variance, not independent error), same caveat as verify_lens; the binding accepts a stronger-model bind as an enhancement.
Notes roles/AGENTS.md (drift home) not yet updated so roles_acceptance will red on drift until the next beat — described as expected, not a defect.
standouts: Adversarial-vs-faithful fire both at confidence 1.0, with the fabrication case naming the exact ungrounded field.
The deliberate choice of a single-generate validator over a draws:N jury, distinguishing it from its quorum-jury siblings.
The empty-field exemption rule that prevents faithful extracts from spuriously failing on schema-default '' fields.
conventions: Follows the seam-lane report schema (with a findings field); mirrors roles/verify_lens.py exactly in shape.
Closed three-field output with a fail-biased posture, matching verify_lens's adversarial-disprove default-to-fail convention.
notes: A build-lane status record: JUDGE added judge_mining as the ③ no-fiction confirm-gate, verified to distinguish a fabricated extract from a grounded one on the resident 4B at confidence 1.0.
questions: Would binding judge_mining to a stronger/independent model materially change the gate's discrimination, or is the 4B sufficient for the mining floor?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — a build report recording the validator role built and how the no-fiction gate was proven by use.
apparent_themes: No-fiction gate as a floor before clustering/drafting memory
Fail-biased validator (false-negative is the dangerous error)
Advisory soft judgement with an honest monoculture caveat
intention_signals: Aims to build the confirm step of the mining pipeline so fabricated extract fields are caught and named before any extract becomes a feedback memory file, while being honest that a single model measures variance not independent error.
novelty: The empty-field exemption combined with a fail-biased single-generate validator, and the explicit positioning as distinct from the draws:N jury siblings.
contribution: Contributes the judge_mining validator definition, its fail-biased no-fiction posture and empty-field exemption rule, and the single-4B monoculture caveat, which a fused system would keep as the provenance of the mining confirm-gate.
summary_for_embedding: A seam-lane JSON report documenting the JUDGE lane's judge_mining role, a single-generate no-fiction confirm-gate for mining extracts, verified to catch fabrication on the resident 4B.
intention_for_embedding: To build the mining pipeline's confirm-gate that flags fabricated extract fields before they become durable feedback memory.
